questions.question: A recipe calls for $3 \cdot 2$ cups of flour to make 6 cupcakes. How many cups are needed for 12 cupcakes?
A construction site has $-5$ degrees Celsius temperature. If it rises by $8$ degrees, what is the new temperature?
Which of the following numbers is an example of an even natural number?
A builder needs to place $15$ bricks in a row, with $3$ bricks for each section. How many sections can be made?
Which of the following patterns will generate only odd numbers: $1, 3, 5, ...$?
A recipe for making cookies calls for $\frac{3}{4}$ cup of sugar. If you want to make $2$ times the recipe, how much sugar is needed?

A construction crew is building a bridge with an arch that has a height of $5$ meters above the water level. The arch is $8$ meters wide, and the crew wants to add a decoration that will hang $2$ meters below the arch. If the current water level is at $-3$ meters, what is the distance from the top of the arch to the water level?
A chef has a recipe for $24$ cupcakes, and she wants to make $2$ batches. If she already has $16$ cupcakes made, how many more cupcakes does she need to make to have $2$ complete batches?

teacher_rules: Allow students to use a calculator for only questions 9 and 10
Have students show all work and explain their reasoning
Remind students that answers must be in simplest form
